description: Tracking information loss across compression levels

ladder.description: Exact operational statements from blind coding
ladder.information_content: Maximum - each response is a unique operational description
examples: A classification scheme was applied to prior studies, separating them into two groups based on what remained stable.
Multiple sequences were generated and the same four features appeared in all of them.
Removal fraction was varied and recovery metrics were measured.

ladder.information_content: High - categories capture main patterns while preserving diversity
ladder.information_lost: Some nuance - individual variations within categories lost

ladder.description: Collapsed into Distinction / Comparison / Preservation / Persistence
ladder.information_lost: One category lost; some methodological nuance lost

ladder.description: Collapsed into Change occurred / No change occurred
ladder.information_lost: Almost all methodological nuance lost; no information about what type of change

information_loss_per_level.loss: Low - some nuance lost but main patterns preserved
Moderate - one category lost, some nuance lost
High - three categories lost, almost all nuance lost

analysis.critical_finding: Compression C loses almost all explanatory structure. The ontology (5 categories) carries real information. But Compression B (4 categories) preserves most structure while being simpler.

operational_compression_ratio.description: Information retained ÷ number of categories. Measures how many studies each category captures on average.
operational_compression_ratio.formula: OCR = Number of studies correctly classified / Number of categories

levels.interpretation: Each category captures 1 study - maximum specificity
Each category captures 4 studies on average - categories are specific
Same as emergent clusters - no additional compression
Each category captures 5 studies - categories are moderately broad
Each category captures 10 studies - categories are very broad

ocr_change_between_levels.interpretation: Large jump - clustering loses significant specificity
Large jump - binary compression loses significant specificity

operational_compression_ratio.key_finding: The biggest OCR jumps are Raw->Emergent (4x) and Compression B->Compression C (2x). The jump from Compression A to Compression B is small (1.25x), suggesting the 4-category and 5-category ontologies carry similar information. The binary ontology (Compression C) is too broad to be useful.

survivor.raw_level: No single response dominates - 20 unique responses
survivor.clustered_level: Generation and Testing (25%) is most common, but no category exceeds 25%
survivor.compression_b: Comparison appears twice (Generation and Testing + Comparison and Reinterpretation), but no single category dominates
survivor.compression_c: All studies show change - no discrimination power
survivor.key_finding: No single category dominates at any level. The archive does not converge on one type of change. The strongest statement is: 'The archive shows multiple types of change, with generation/testing being slightly more common.'
